overall_note: Only one revision (E, 25/10/21) of each sheet present — no superseded duplicates to exclude. The Electrical Plan (106) is extremely dense: GPO/data/comm triangle symbols are tiny and heavily overlapped by dimension strings and mounting-height callouts (e.g. (300),(600),(1000)), making precise discrimination of single vs double vs USB/cleaning variants unreliable — most power/data counts are LOW confidence and should be field-verified. The RCP (102) light-fitting counts are approximations because the plan is small-scale with overlapping L-codes and dashed switching lines; reflected ceiling symbols (EM, L1-L9) could not all be individually resolved. Float-room services (E1/E2/AS/HEAT/15amp) are the most reliable as they are clearly labelled per room. Exit/emergency quantities are partly governed by a note deferring to centre management, so actual counts may differ.
